description: Three-state stop conditions for v112T free-source one-shot plan. These rules determine whether a live source response triggers CONTINUE, ABORT, or DEGRADE_TO_MOCK. All rules are evaluated in order; the first matching condition determines the outcome. ABORT is checked first, then DEGRADE, then CONTINUE as the fallback.

ABORT.description: Hard stop conditions. If ANY of these are true, the entire response is rejected. No mock fallback is produced. The v112T plan records the abort reason.

rules.condition: HTTP response status code is not in [200, 201, 202, 203, 204]
rules.note: Includes 3xx redirects (not expected from REST API), 4xx client errors, 5xx server errors.

rules.condition: HTTP response status code is 429 (Too Many Requests)
rules.note: Rate limited. Do not retry automatically. v112U may add backoff with user confirmation.

rules.condition: Single request takes longer than 15 seconds to complete
rules.note: Timeout per-request, not cumulative. CoinGecko free tier may be slow during high load.

rules.condition: Total wall-clock time for all requests exceeds 120 seconds
rules.note: Cumulative timeout. Prevents runaway planning loops.

rules.condition: Response body cannot be parsed as valid JSON
rules.note: Includes truncated responses, HTML error pages, empty bodies.

rules.condition: Parsed JSON does not match expected response schema (missing required top-level keys, wrong types)
rules.note: Schema validation against LiveSourceResponse schema. Structural mismatch only — missing optional fields go to DEGRADE.

rules.condition: CoinGecko price vs CoinCap price for the same asset differs by > 5% (when both sources return data)
rules.note: Absolute percentage difference: |price_cg - price_cc| / max(price_cg, price_cc) > 0.05. Indicates data quality issue.

rules.condition: Max timestamp difference between any two asset observations > 120 seconds
rules.note: Stale data risk. Timestamps from the source's last_updated / timestamp field.

rules.condition: More than 20% of required_fields are missing across all assets in the response
rules.note: Required fields: asset_id, symbol, price_usd, price_change_pct, last_updated_at. Missing rate > 0.20 triggers ABORT.

DEGRADE_TO_MOCK.description: Soft degradation conditions. The response is usable but quality is insufficient for real send. The adapter converts the live response to mock envelope format, marking it as DEGRADE_TO_MOCK and ineligible for real send.

rules.condition: Some but not all requested assets returned successfully, and success rate < 80%
rules.note: If CoinGecko returns 7/10 assets, that's 70% < 80% → DEGRADE. The successful assets are still processed.

rules.condition: One or more optional_fields are missing or null in the response
rules.note: Optional fields include open_interest_change_pct, source_latency_ms. Missing optional fields are acceptable but reduce confidence.

rules.condition: Any asset metric is within ±10% of the v112Q threshold boundary
rules.note: Example: if min_per_asset_abs_price_change_pct is 2.0, and an asset shows 1.8-2.2% change, it's in the boundary zone. In a real live scenario, this could flip with a small price move.

rules.condition: Source data freshness is degraded: last_updated_at > 120s from fetched_at, or source reports stale cache
rules.note: CoinGecko free tier caches data for 30-60s. If data is older than 120s, freshness is degraded.

rules.condition: Only one source returned data (the other failed, timed out, or returned no matching assets)
rules.note: Cross-validation impossible with single source. The signal cannot be verified independently.

CONTINUE.description: Conditions for a successful response that can proceed to mock adapter processing. CONTINUE does NOT mean real_send is allowed — eligible_for_real_send remains false per v112T policy.

rules.condition: All required_fields are present and non-null for every asset in the response
rules.note: asset_id, symbol, price_usd, price_change_pct, last_updated_at must all be present.

rules.condition: When both CoinGecko and CoinCap return data for the same asset, price divergence < 2%
rules.note: Tight consensus threshold for high-confidence signals.

rules.condition: Max timestamp difference between any two observations < 60 seconds
rules.note: This aligns with v112Q max_timestamp_skew_seconds = 60.

rules.condition: All v112Q stricter thresholds pass (direction agreement, per-asset price filter, secondary metric, timestamp skew, leader detection, sector concentration, volume outlier)
rules.note: v112Q thresholds are evaluated with available data. If OI is missing, only volume serves as secondary metric.

rules.condition: No ABORT or DEGRADE_TO_MOCK conditions have been triggered
rules.note: This is the default fallback when all checks above pass.

rules.condition: eligible_for_real_send MUST be false regardless of CONTINUE status
rules.note: This is a policy-level constraint, not a data-quality constraint. Even a perfect CONTINUE response must not be eligible for real send in v112T.

rate_limit_and_fallback_strategy.description: Planned rate limit handling and fallback chain. Not executed in v112T — defined for v112U.
rate_limit_and_fallback_strategy.retry_justification: Free tier APIs may interpret retries as abuse. v112U will prompt user before any retry.
fallback_chain: 2. If CoinGecko fails with non-2xx or timeout, try CoinCap /v2/assets (fallback)
3. If both fail, ABORT — no data available
4. If both succeed but disagree > 5%, ABORT (price divergence)
5. If only one succeeds, DEGRADE_TO_MOCK (multi-source uncertain)
6. If both succeed and agree < 2%, proceed to CONTINUE path
rate_limit_and_fallback_strategy.concurrency_note: Requests to different sources MAY be parallelized in v112U to reduce total duration. v112T defines this but does not execute it.

state_write_policy.description: v112T does NOT write any production state.
